text: This is a patient with a left-sided malignant pleural mesothelioma.
The patient is in full lateral decubitus position.
We perform a large single-skin incision along the sixth rib since we prefer a second basalt
The previous warts incision should be in the line of the trichotomy incision.
The previous warts incision is resected in full thickness till the pleura is bleached.
This tissue can be resected, otherwise it could be placed at the pleura as en bloc specimen.
Afterwards the sixth intercostal space is opened.
We do not resect ribs but we perform a posterior costotomy to facilitate the extraclural dissection.
This is a posterior costotomy.
Afterwards an extra pleural dissection plane is created bluntly and sharply.
As shown here the inter thoracic fascia as well as the periosteum of the ribs are dissected en bloc.
Otherwise piecemeal removal is possible.
A nasocastric tube as a leading landmark might avoid esophageal injuries at this point.
Here is a view on the dissected aorta and the spared left vagal nerve.
Basal trochotomy is performed in the 9th intercostal space through the same skin incision.
The basalt teracotomy allows superb exposure of the diaphragm and the costophrenic angle.
Then the intercostal space is opened in the usual manner.
Afterwards, the extraplural dissection could be completed till the diaphragm is reached caudally.
The radical plurectomy technique usually allows the isolated resection of the diaphragmatic pleura without impairment of the diaphragmatic function.
The pleural sac is kept closed and dissected bluntly and sharply from the underlying diaphragmatic muscle fibers.
In case of an infiltration of the diaphragm, which could be detected during dissection, partial or complete resection of the diaphragm can be performed immediately.
In this presented case, the tumour extension involves the diaphragm muscle fibres.
Through the basal toracotomy, complete resection of the diaphragm is performed.
Dissection from the peritoneum is facilitated through this incision.
All attempts should be made to avoid tumour spread in the webbed domain.
is one of the most important and difficult parts of the surgery.
Positive and expiratory pressure is applied on the lung and adjusted as needed to facilitate the dissection of the visceral pleura and to distinguish between lung and pleura.
This can be very challenging.
I usually start with a PEEP level of 10 cm of H2O.
Then the parietal and visceral pleura is incised with a blade very precisely.
Both sheets of the pleura are kept together.
Then the visceral pleuroctomy proceeds with sharp dissection using a scalpel or scissors as well as blunt dissection
using fingers, small sponge stick or other grasping instruments.
The initial steps can be very time consuming.
However, it is very important to get an optimal dissection plane.
Once the dissection plane is created, a grasping instrument like a cop dissector might accelerate the dissection.
Once the lung hilum is reached, the pleura is incised and the dissection is carried on.
After the upper lobe is completely freed, then the visceral pleurectomy from the lower lobe starts in the same fashion.
Both sheets of the pleura are incised with the scalpel till the lung parenchyma is reached.
I use the scissors to create an optimal dissection plane in the lower lobe as well.
The osteoporactomy is carried on in the same fashion as in the upper lobe.
At this point, the fissure is freed from the lower lobe.
Once the fissure is freed from the upper and lower lobe, the PEEP level is reduced
and the pleura is dissected from the underlying interlobar branches of the pulmonary artery.
Now the fissure is completely freed from the pleura.
The last attachment and the connective tissue is dissected.
Finally,
The en bloc specimen with the pleural sac, lung parenchyma, diaphragm, mediastinal and pleural fat is removed from the thoracic cavity.
Microscopic complete resection is accomplished.
This view shows the tumor volume which was resected during the last four and a half hours.
Finally the left lung is inflated.
Both lobes are fully expanded.
